feed.title: al-ice.ai — AI security intelligence
feed.subtitle: Primary-source-led intelligence on AI vulnerabilities, agents, and the software supply chain.

entry.title: NIST Finds GLM-5.2 Cyber Capability Near Opus 4.6—but Safeguards Allow Exploit Development
entry.summary: NIST CAISI assessed open-weight GLM-5.2 as comparable to Opus 4.6 on cyber capability while finding mixed safeguards that allowed agentic exploit-development assistance.

entry.title: ToolHive Auth Discovery Escaped MCP Container Isolation via Host-Side SSRF — CVE-2026-58196
entry.summary: ToolHive before 0.31.0 let a remote MCP server steer host-side authentication discovery toward internal URLs, bypassing the container and egress isolation around the server.

entry.title: Hugging Face Says an Autonomous Agent Breached Its Dataset Pipeline
entry.summary: Hugging Face disclosed an AI-driven intrusion that began with malicious dataset processing, reached internal clusters, and generated more than 17,000 recorded events.

entry.title: OpenAI’s GPT-Red Uses Self-Play to Find Prompt Injections Before Deployment
entry.summary: OpenAI trained an internal attacker model through self-play, then used its prompt-injection discoveries to harden GPT-5.6; the results show both the promise and limits of automated red-teaming.

entry.title: AWS HealthLake MCP Pagination SSRF Could Exfiltrate Temporary Credentials — CVE-2026-15643
entry.summary: AWS HealthLake MCP Server before 0.0.14 trusted a caller-supplied pagination URL, allowing an authenticated user to redirect a signed request and expose temporary AWS credentials.

entry.title: MCP Documentation Server Exposed Its Knowledge Base Without Authentication — CVE-2026-54504
entry.summary: mcp-documentation-server 1.13.0 bound its Web UI and document API to every interface without authentication, exposing corpus read, search, insertion, and deletion operations.

entry.title: Strands Agents Memory Tool Let Prompts Redirect an Elasticsearch API Key — CVE-2026-15746
entry.summary: Strands Agents Tools before 0.7.0 exposed Elasticsearch connection fields to the model, allowing a crafted prompt to redirect an operator API key to an attacker-controlled host.

entry.title: n8n-MCP Cross-Tenant Backup Flaws Expose Workflow Secrets — CVE-2026-54052 and CVE-2026-55608
entry.summary: n8n-mcp 2.57.3 and earlier can expose local workflow backups across tenant boundaries in shared HTTP deployments; version 2.57.4 closes both disclosed paths.

entry.title: Grok Build 0.2.93 Uploaded Whole Git Repositories Before Server-Side Shutdown
entry.summary: Wire-level testing found Grok Build 0.2.93 uploaded tracked files and full Git history independently of agent reads. xAI has disabled the upload server-side and promised deletion.

entry.title: json_repair Circular Schema Reference Can Pin AI Service Workers — GHSA-xf7x-x43h-rpqh
entry.summary: json-repair 0.59.10 and earlier can enter an infinite CPU loop when an untrusted JSON Schema contains a circular $ref; version 0.60.1 fixes the high-severity DoS.

entry.title: MCP Atlassian 0.22 Closes a 37-Advisory Security Audit
entry.summary: mcp-atlassian 0.22.0 fixes a coordinated set of flaws spanning server-side file reads, HTTP authentication, SSRF, tool authorization, filters, OAuth storage, and XSS; 0.22.1 closes follow-up gaps.

entry.title: MemGhost Turns One Email Into Persistent Agent Memory Poisoning
entry.summary: MemGhost achieved 87.5% end-to-end stealth memory-injection success against OpenClaw with GPT-5.4 on 56 held-out cases, showing how one email can corrupt future agent behavior.

entry.title: Tracebit Context Bombs Turn Model Guardrails Against Offensive AI Agents
entry.summary: Tracebit reports that a guardrail-triggering string planted in one decoy AWS secret cut AI agents’ admin-success rate from 57% to 5% across 152 attack runs.

entry.title: Cloudflare Precursor Moves Agent Detection From Requests to Full Sessions
entry.summary: Cloudflare’s Precursor continuously evaluates browser behavior across a session, feeding agent and bot signals into bot scores, challenges, analytics, and WAF policy.

entry.title: Ghostcommit Hides Secret-Theft Instructions in Images AI Code Reviewers Never Open
entry.summary: UMKC researchers demonstrate Ghostcommit, a pull-request attack that hides prompt injection in a PNG, bypasses text-only review, and makes coding agents encode .env secrets into source code.

entry.title: Prismata Cuts Web-Agent Prompt-Injection Success from 85.5% to 0.7%
entry.summary: UC Berkeley researchers propose Prismata, a DOM-aware least-privilege layer that confines web agents and sharply reduces cross-site prompt-injection success in WebArena tests.

entry.title: AWS MCP Gateway Registry SQL Injection Exposes API Key Material — CVE-2026-14471
entry.summary: AWS patched CVE-2026-14471, a high-severity authenticated SQL injection in MCP Gateway Registry versions 1.0.3 through 1.0.12 that can expose API key material.

entry.title: Google Cloud Run Sandboxes Put Agent-Generated Code Behind Deny-by-Default Egress
entry.summary: Google has put Cloud Run sandboxes into public preview, adding credential isolation, default-deny network egress, and disposable filesystem overlays for AI-generated code.

entry.title: arXiv — SpellSmith: Text-Based Shield for MCP Taint-Style Vulnerabilities
entry.summary: Tongji researchers propose SpellSmith, a text-based mitigation that embeds behavioral guardrails into MCP tool descriptions to block taint-style exploits without code-level patches.

entry.title: Ethereum Foundation AI Agent Audit — CVE-2026-34219 Gossipsub DoS
entry.summary: Ethereum Foundation AI Agent Audit — CVE-2026-34219 Gossipsub DoS

entry.title: Microsoft — Claude Code GitHub Action Exposes CI/CD Secrets via /proc/self/environ
entry.summary: Microsoft — Claude Code GitHub Action Exposes CI/CD Secrets via /proc/self/environ

entry.title: Microsoft MDASH — AI Agentic Scanning Finds 16 Windows CVEs in First Run
entry.summary: Microsoft MDASH — AI Agentic Scanning Finds 16 Windows CVEs in First Run

entry.title: OpenClaw AI Agent — WhatsApp Message to Host RCE via Sandbox Bypass
entry.summary: Three high-severity vulnerabilities in OpenClaw let attackers chain environment variable injection, git transport abuse, and Docker sandbox escape to achieve host-level code execution via WhatsApp messages.

entry.title: HalluSquatting — Agentic Botnets via LLM Hallucinated Resource Names
entry.summary: Tel Aviv University researchers demonstrate how attackers can weaponize LLM hallucinations to establish agentic botnets, achieving 100% attack success against multiple AI coding assistants.

entry.title: CrowdStrike Prompt Injection Taxonomy — 200+ Techniques Cataloged
entry.summary: CrowdStrike expands its prompt injection taxonomy to over 200 techniques, revealing how attacks against AI agents are fragmenting into composite chains.

entry.title: Unit 42 — Pickle in the Middle: Cross-Tenant RCE in Google Vertex AI
entry.summary: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 disclosed a critical vulnerability in Google Cloud Vertex AI SDK that allowed cross-tenant model poisoning and remote code execution via bucket squatting.

entry.title: CISA — Langflow Becomes First AI Agent Platform in KEV Catalog (CVE-2026-55255)
entry.summary: CISA adds Langflow CVE-2026-55255 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog — the first AI agent orchestration platform ever listed — with a July 10 deadline.

entry.title: arXiv — GitHub Copilot Refuses Harmful Requests in Chat, Writes Them in Code
entry.summary: New research demonstrates workflow-level jailbreak construction: coding agents that refuse harmful prompts in chat will produce the same harmful content when asked to write it as code.

entry.title: ESET — 3,000+ Malicious AI Skills Found in Agent Ecosystem Scan
entry.summary: ESET — 3,000+ Malicious AI Skills Found in Agent Ecosystem Scan

entry.title: European Commission — EU Action Plan on Cybersecurity and AI
entry.summary: The EU published an Action Plan on July 7, 2026 to address risks and opportunities of advanced AI for cybersecurity, including AI model evaluation, structured access, and secure testing platforms.

entry.title: Fiddler AI — AI Coding Agent Threat Models and Controls
entry.summary: Fiddler AI maps the full coding-agent attack surface — prompt injection, supply-chain poisoning, credential leaks, MCP spoofing — and proposes runtime guardrails.

entry.title: When Your Software Supply Chain Includes AI Writing Your Code
entry.summary: When Your Software Supply Chain Includes AI Writing Your Code

entry.title: Sygnia — Lone Attacker Uses Agentic AI to Compromise AWS in 72 Hours
entry.summary: Sygnia reports a single threat actor used agentic AI workflows to execute a full cloud compromise in 72 hours — a campaign that would have taken weeks manually.

entry.title: TeamPCP — Supply Chain Compromise of Trivy, LiteLLM, and KICS Feeds VECT Ransomware Credential Archive
entry.summary: TeamPCP poisoned Trivy, Checkmarx KICS, LiteLLM, and Telnyx SDK to harvest 500K+ CI/CD credentials — VECT ransomware then selected victims from the archive.

entry.title: arXiv — Agent Data Injection (ADI): New IPI Category Bypasses All Defenses
entry.summary: Seoul National University and UIUC researchers introduce Agent Data Injection, a new class of indirect prompt injection that exploits lack of trusted/untrusted data isolation in LLM agents.

entry.title: CVE-2026-59707 — LocalAI Unauthenticated SSRF in Model Apply Endpoint
entry.summary: LocalAI before the latest patch contains an unauthenticated SSRF vulnerability in POST /models/apply that allows attackers to fetch arbitrary internal URLs.

entry.title: GitLost — GitHub Agentic Workflows Leak Private Repos via Prompt Injection
entry.summary: Noma Labs discovered GitLost, a critical indirect prompt injection in GitHub Agentic Workflows that lets unauthenticated attackers exfiltrate private repository content.

entry.title: Langroid CVE-2026-55615 — Prompt-to-Cypher Injection Enables RCE via Neo4j
entry.summary: Langroid CVE-2026-55615 — Prompt-to-Cypher Injection Enables RCE via Neo4j

entry.title: vLLM Patches Two New DoS CVEs in v0.24.0 — Audio Memory Exhaustion and Regex ReDoS
entry.summary: CVE-2026-55646 lets unauthenticated API callers exhaust memory via oversized audio uploads; CVE-2026-55574 enables indefinite inference worker hangs via adversarial regex patterns.

entry.title: vLLM CVE-2026-55646 — Audio Upload Memory Exhaustion DoS (CVSS 6.5)
entry.summary: vLLM speech-to-text endpoints allocate full upload before enforcing audio file-size limit, enabling remote memory exhaustion DoS. Fixed in 0.24.0.

entry.title: Zscaler — Indirect Prompt Injection Tricks AI Agents Into Crypto Payments
entry.summary: Two campaigns use SEO poisoning and indirect prompt injection to manipulate AI agents into making cryptocurrency payments or trusting fraudulent DeFi platforms.

entry.title: AI Bug Hunting Drives Record CVE Spike — 1,500 High-Severity Flaws in June 2026
entry.summary: June 2026 set a record for high- and top-severity CVEs among 21 notable organizations, driven by AI-powered vulnerability discovery tools like Claude Mythos and GPT-5.5-Cyber.

entry.title: FatFs: LLM-Assisted Fuzzing Finds 7 CVEs in Embedded Firmware
entry.summary: runZero researchers used GitHub Copilot to fuzz FatFs, a compact C library for FAT/exFAT media parsing, discovering 7 CVEs affecting IoT devices, drones, ATMs, and voting machines.

entry.title: Microsoft Execution Containers (MXC) — Sandboxing AI Agents on Windows
entry.summary: Microsoft introduces Execution Containers (MXC), a new security layer for containing AI agent workflows on Windows with policy-driven isolation and identity enforcement.

entry.title: NVIDIA Releases SkillSpector — Open-Source Scanner for AI Agent Skills
entry.summary: NVIDIA open-sourced SkillSpector, a security scanner that detects vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and supply-chain risks in AI agent skills before installation.

entry.title: TrendAI Audit Finds 4,982 Security Flaws Across 2,259 Public MCP Servers
entry.summary: TrendAI analysis of 9,695 MCP servers found 4,982 security issues across 2,259 servers, debunking assumptions about popularity and verification badges.

entry.title: Vercel Breach: Shadow AI Tool Becomes Identity-Based Supply Chain Pivot
entry.summary: Vercel Breach: Shadow AI Tool Becomes Identity-Based Supply Chain Pivot

entry.title: Adversa AI — 27 Agentic AI Security Resources for July 2026
entry.summary: Adversa AI — 27 Agentic AI Security Resources for July 2026

entry.title: Check Point — Critical Vulnerability Exposures Double in 2026, AI-Driven Triage Urgent
entry.summary: Check Point — Critical Vulnerability Exposures Double in 2026, AI-Driven Triage Urgent

entry.title: China — AI Companion Regulation Forces ByteDance and Alibaba to Kill Agent Features
entry.summary: China — AI Companion Regulation Forces ByteDance and Alibaba to Kill Agent Features
